figures.caption: Fig. 5. Representative images from DRIVE (left), STARE (centre), and CHASE_DB1 (right), with ground truth vessel annotations overlaid. The difference in vessel contrast, image uniformity, and thin vessel visibility is apparent across the three datasets. Mean Dice in Fold 1 is 91.4% on DRIVE, 88.4% on STARE, and 87.2% on CHASE_DB1.
Fig. 6. 5-fold stratified cross-validation scheme with exact image counts per fold.

Fig. 14. Close-up of the primary failure region in CHASE_DB1 Image_13R (Dice 82.57%). Left panel shows the raw fundus patch at increased magnification. Centre shows the ground truth annotation. Right shows the model prediction.
Table 8. Comparison between HMS-VesselNet v2 and v3 across architecture, training, and evaluation properties.
Fig. 8. Training curves for all five cross-validation folds showing training loss and validation Dice over epochs.
Fig. 12. Fusion weight convergence trajectories for all five cross-validation folds. Each subplot corresponds to one-fold.

Fig. 1. Representative fundus image patches illustrating the range of segmentation difficulty. Left two patches show major and mid-sized vessels from DRIVE and STARE, where vessel boundaries are well defined and contrast is high. Right two patches show thin peripheral vessels from CHASE_DB1, where vessel width falls below 2 pixels and contrast is significantly lower.
Fig. 3. Architecture of a single Attention U-Net branch.
Table 6. Leave-one-dataset-out results for three experiments with cross-validation mean shown for reference.
Fig. 13. Per-image Dice distribution for the three LODO experiments. Each point represents one test image.
Table 1: Summary of the three datasets used in this study. All images are 8-bit RGB with manual vessel annotations provided by the respective dataset authors.
Table 2. Per-fold and mean cross-validation results under 8-orientation TTA on the held-out test set. Val N is the number of held-out images per fold.
Fig. 10. Failure cases from the hardest CHASE_DB1 images across cross-validation folds.

Fig. 7. Example segmentation output on a CHASE_DB1 peripheral patch. (a) Raw fundus image. (b) Ground truth vessel annotation. (c) Predicted segmentation with error map: false negatives in red, false positives in blue.

Table 4. Ablation study on Fold 1 held-out set under 4-orientation TTA. A0 is the single-scale plain U-Net baseline. A1 adds attention gates. A2 is the full HMS-VesselNet v3 evaluated under 4-TTA. Sensitivity is the primary comparison metric. The A2 result under 8-TTA is 88.94%, confirming that the TTA variant does not alter the relative conclusions.

Fig. 9. Predicted segmentations against ground truth for three representative cases from Fold 1. Top row: DRIVE 36_training (Dice 92.11%). Middle row: STARE im0001 (Dice 90.45%). Bottom row: CHASE_DB1 Image_13R (Dice 82.57%). Columns show the raw fundus image, ground truth annotation, model prediction, and error map with false negatives in red and false positives in blue. False negatives in the bottom row are concentrated at thin peripheral branches.
Table 7. Comparison of published methods on DRIVE and CHASE_DB1. * Dice reported as F1 score in the original paper. † Result reported on a non-standard split or subset
Fig. 4. Hard example mining schedule.
Fig. 11. Convergence of the four learned fusion weights over training epochs for Fold 1.
Fig. 2. Overall architecture of HMS-VesselNet.
Table 3. Per-image Dice and Sensitivity for all 14 held-out images in Fold 1 grouped by dataset.
